context: Checkpoint 2026-04-30T13:00Z after Studio surface activation ledger and apps/dev runtime refactor slices. Branch develop is clean and ahead of origin/develop by 2 local commits before this handoff update. Latest pushed remote HEAD is 60093f8e and GitHub Actions run 25165702645 completed successfully. Disk remains constrained but stable at about 9.4GB free; repo is about 1.6GB and node_modules about 975MB, so no cleanup was run because current artifacts are small and rebuild cost would be higher than reclaimed space.
current_tasks: Remote checkpoints since prior handoff: pushed Homestead surface trust gate through c8493e10 and CI run 25165024862 passed; pushed Studio /surfaces ledger through 60093f8e and CI run 25165702645 passed.
Current local commits not yet pushed before this handoff update: 4f29d509 refactor(app-dev): centralize studio plugin handles; afd9fa0d refactor(app-dev): extract surface diagnostics fixtures.

`apps/dev/src/lib/studio-plugin.ts` now centralizes local Studio PluginInstance handle creation. Default entry is `internal:<id>` so Homestead's trust gate can distinguish explicit internal Studio fixtures from registry-governed external plugins. Tests are in `apps/dev/src/lib/studio-plugin.test.ts`.

`apps/dev/src/lib/stream-demo.ts`, dashboard plugin registration in `apps/dev/src/pages/index.astro`, and `/surfaces` now use `createStudioPluginHandle(...)` instead of duplicating PluginInstance boilerplate.

`apps/dev/src/lib/surface-diagnostics.ts` now owns the `/surfaces` diagnostics fixtures: one trusted internal surface plugin (`studio-surface-diagnostics`) and one external untrusted fixture (`external-untrusted-surface`). Tests are in `apps/dev/src/lib/surface-diagnostics.test.ts`.

`apps/dev/src/pages/surfaces.astro` remains the dedicated Studio surface activation ledger. It boots a small diagnostics Tractor runtime, registers the diagnostics fixtures, runs Homestead shell setup, and renders mounted/rejected diagnostics through Homestead inspector helpers.

Prior completed path remains intact: manifest `extensions.surfaces`, Homestead slot/kind/capability/trust gating, DOM identity, `ui:surface_mounted` and `ui:surface_rejected` telemetry, Studio statusbar diagnostics, `/surfaces` ledger, opt-in stream demo plugin, DS primitive adoption for generic visuals, and Homestead stream rendering from `StreamSession`/`StreamChunk`.

Domain boundary remains: Homestead owns surface semantics, activation planning, stream rendering primitives, and diagnostic normalization; apps/dev owns Studio composition/experiments/diagnostic UI; @refarm.dev/ds owns only framework-agnostic visual primitives; BrowserSyncClient remains schema-neutral.

Validation passed after local refactor slices: `npm --prefix apps/dev test`, `npm --prefix apps/dev run type-check`, `npm --prefix apps/dev run build`, `npm run agent:streaming:studio`, `npm run project:validate --silent`, and `git diff --check`. Build still emits known Vite browser-externalization warnings for Node modules in registry/tractor/heartwood, but completes successfully.
blockers: Disk is still 99% used on the host mount, but free space is stable around 9.4GB. The repo is about 1.6GB; current generated artifacts are not large enough to justify cleanup. Recheck disk before broad builds and clean only if free space falls near 2-3GB or new large artifacts appear.
Rust target dirs were previously removed by clean:heavy. Broad Rust checks will rebuild from scratch and may consume disk. Use scoped Rust commands from AGENTS.md only if Rust is touched.
Full tractor-ts unit suite can be noisy when WASM fixtures are missing after cleanup. Prefer focused tests or aggregate npm agent scripts unless intentionally rebuilding fixtures.

`apps/dev` importing from the Homestead SDK barrel can pull `Shell` and locale imports through dist. For inspector-only consumers use explicit subpath `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk/surface-inspector`. Pages that boot `StudioShell` necessarily use `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk`.
Audit moderate findings remain known and non-critical: Astro, DOMPurify, PostCSS, uuid transitive chains. No high/critical vulnerabilities in pre-push/CI audits.
next_actions: First command after compaction: `git status --branch --short && df -h /workspaces/refarm && git log --oneline -12`.
If continuing local work before push, run economical touched-area gates: `npm run agent:streaming:studio && npm run project:validate --silent && git diff --check`.
Push current local commits after handoff if not already pushed, then watch CI. Current local stack before handoff update was ahead 2: 4f29d509 and afd9fa0d.

Next best slice: extract a small apps/dev Studio Tractor boot helper for OPFS/Loro/identity setup used by dashboard and `/surfaces`, but keep it in apps/dev and avoid pulling domain behavior into DS. Consider a pure helper test first to avoid browser runtime coupling.

Next diagnostics slice: improve `/surfaces` with richer fields (trust source, registry status, missing capabilities) as structured columns instead of only text in the reused statusbar inspector component.
Next extensibility slice: create a small executable multi-surface example only if needed; examples should remain under `examples/` until executable code/build scripts justify a workspace package.
Next DS slice: only move genuinely agnostic visual styling into @refarm.dev/ds; do not move Homestead surface semantics or Studio diagnostics behavior into DS.
Run `npm run clean:light --silent` only at checkpoint boundaries if `.turbo`, dist, or validation artifacts grow meaningfully. Avoid heavy cleanup unless disk pressure is critical.
open_questions: Should dashboard and `/surfaces` share a real `bootStudioTractor(...)` helper, or is current duplication acceptable until another Studio page needs Tractor boot?
Should the `/surfaces` ledger render a table/structured graph distinct from the compact statusbar inspector, while still using Homestead semantic helpers?
Should `unknown-slot` rejection also apply to legacy `ui.slots`, or should legacy stay ignored for compatibility as implemented?
Which Homestead surface kinds should be accepted by default long-term? Current allow-list is `panel`, `widget`, `statusbar`, `editor`.
Where should deeper runtime trust be enforced long-term: before DOM wrapper creation in Homestead, inside PluginHost activation, or both with different responsibilities?
Should `ui.slots` be formally deprecated after one executable plugin-provided Homestead surface proves the new model?
key_decisions_pending: Whether to push the latest local apps/dev refactor commits before further work.
Runtime trust boundary for manifest-declared Homestead surfaces beyond current registry/internal-entry gate.
First real executable multi-surface plugin example location: `examples/` vs workspace package.
Whether daily-driver parity checklist should be converted into canonical .project tasks after current surface path stabilizes.
